reason: Modifying GRUB bootloader configuration to change the superuser username involves editing /etc/grub.d/01_users and regenerating grub.cfg. Incorrect syntax or missing export line can render the system unbootable. According to policy, bootloader changes are classified as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; bootloader misconfiguration; system may fail to boot if grub.cfg is not generated correctly; incorrect syntax in /etc/grub.d/01_users; missing export superusers line
